featured.title: Camera test chart company DSC Labs reborn
featured.teaser: Digital imaging technician and image workflow specialist Matt Conrad acquired DSC Labs last summer and has been appointed CEO

featured.title: Report: Sports rights holders need to diversify beyond live content
featured.teaser: Altman Solon's latest survey found sports streaming has overtaken linear in Spain and Italy and is approaching parity in the US The post Report: Sports rights holders need to diversify beyond live content appeared first on TVBEurope .

featured.title: Changing the conversation on inclusive production
featured.teaser: A recent RTS Technology Centre event brought together production innovators and industry specialists to examine how accessible design and inclusive production practices are reshaping the way content is made, and where the industry still needs to move (much) faster

featured.title: Sir Roger Deakins among 2026 NAB Show speakers
featured.teaser: Organisers have announced a number of speakers and sessions spanning the creator economy, filmmaking, streaming platforms, artificial intelligence, broadcast technology and journalism

featured.title: Opinion: Just because we can…
featured.teaser: Matthew Corrigan wonders if we are all guilty of talking a good game on sustainability

featured.title: Report: IP workflows boost live production as satellite and fibre declines
featured.teaser: The report found satellite and fibre networks are declining with IP-based workflows enabling live production to be more scalable and responsive The post Report: IP workflows boost live production as satellite and fibre declines appeared first on TVBEurope .

featured.title: Audio-Technica debuts on-camera shotgun microphones
featured.teaser: The AVT-SG1 and AVT-SG1LE have been designed for professional videographers and content creators The post Audio-Technica debuts on-camera shotgun microphones appeared first on TVBEurope .

featured.title: Accedo One and Magine Pro’s Leyra delivers unified OTT SaaS platform
featured.teaser: The platform has been designed to enable OTT providers to boost audience engagement and retention The post Accedo One and Magine Pro’s Leyra delivers unified OTT SaaS platform appeared first on TVBEurope .

featured.title: Garden Studios drives virtual production capabilities forward
featured.teaser: The stage layout reduces vehicle repositioning, with approximately 90 per cent of camera angles achievable using a single 180-degree rotation The post Garden Studios drives virtual production capabilities forward appeared first on TVBEurope .

featured.title: YouTube to stream first ten minutes of FIFA World Cup matches
featured.teaser: The 'innovative agreement' aims to enhance viewer experiences and unlock monetisation opportunities with premium content across FIFA and its Media Partners’ respective official YouTube channels The post YouTube to stream first ten minutes of FIFA World Cup matches appeared first

featured.title: Lisa Nandy suggests BBC could be given permanent charter

featured.title: Vislink debuts DragonFly V 5G dual-modem video transmitter

featured.title: Matrox Video’s encoders/decoders built into Rai’s IP workflow

featured.title: Larissa Görner-Meeus joins Net Insight

featured.title: Sam Tewunga moves into CEO role at UKTV

featured.title: AI standardisation speeds up

featured.title: WMSA Europe aims to tackle regulatory pressure on audio PMSE spectrum

streaming.title: Broadpeak Debuts “Best of Both Worlds” Multi-Package Multiview
streaming.teaser: I recently interviewed Damien Sterkers, VP of Products and Solutions Marketing at Broadpeak, to discuss the company’s new multiview solution for live streaming. Briefly, Broadpeak has developed an innovative server-side approach that delivers the best of both worlds: the universa

streaming.title: The Next Big Feature in Live Streaming: VMAF-Driven Bitrate Control
streaming.teaser: For most of the live encoding era, the operator’s main job was picking a bitrate. You’d set a target, the encoder would hit it, and quality was whatever came out the other end. Simple content looked great. Complex content looked terrible. The encoder didn’t care because you told

streaming.title: Contextual Targeting’s Second Act: Past, Present, and Future
streaming.teaser: Contextual advertising matches ads to content rather than to people. The logic is straightforward: a viewer watching content about home renovation is likely receptive to a Home Depot ad at that moment, whereas a behavioral retarget from last week’s browsing is not. The problem is

streaming.title: Capped CRF in a Multi-Codec World: FFmpeg and NVIDIA Implementations
streaming.teaser: I recently consulted with a company that was running capped CRF-type encoding across four codecs simultaneously: x264, VP9, NVIDIA H.264, and NVIDIA AV1. The first two were VOD encoding, the second two live. I was genuinely impressed by the sophistication and practicality of this

streaming.title: David Ronca Details VCAT Beta 4.0 with VVC Decode
streaming.teaser: On February 12, I interviewed David Ronca, formerly of Netflix and Meta, now RoncaTech, about the latest Beta version of his Video Codec Acid Test, or VCAT, which now includes VVC decode courtesy of Fraunhofer’s open-source VVeC decoder. The interview is available here on YouTube

streaming.title: Lies, Damn Lies, and Estimated Bandwidth Savings
streaming.teaser: When a codec researcher declares that a newer codec is up to 40% more efficient than the baseline codec, they are making a highly accurate, well-defined claim. However, much like the EPA fuel economy ratings on a new car’s window sticker, these white papers should include a discl

streaming.title: My Three Take Evolution in Vibe Coding
streaming.teaser: The marketing story around vibe coding is awe-inspiring: a non‑programmer can describe any app they can imagine, and AI will do all the hard work. No code, no problem. Just vibes. It turns out I was vibe coding before I ever heard the term. My goals were both less and more ambiti

streaming.title: Per‑Title Before New Codecs: Fixing Your H.264 Baseline
streaming.teaser: Before comparing your existing H.264 encodes to HEVC, AV1, or any other advanced codec, you need a baseline you can trust. In practice, many codec efficiency claims collapse once you examine how inefficient the underlying H.264 ladder actually is. This article focuses on fixing t

streaming.title: Beyond H.264. Why Codec Choices Now Carry Legal and Financial Risk
streaming.teaser: Codec decisions used to be an engineering sport. Today, they are capital allocation decisions with legal risk attached. For most of the H.264 era, codec choices were technical and reversible: you ran some tests, watched the BD‑rate curves, and rolled out what looked best on your

streaming.title: Netflix’s Masterclass: Engineering Live Streaming at Scale
streaming.teaser: As a technologist and part-time couch potato, I appreciate Netflix on many levels. During the evening hours, it’s the creativity and breathtaking beauty of its productions. During working hours, it’s their technical contributions, like per-title encoding and VMAF, and their devel
